source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A light detector has an absorbing area of \( 2.00\times10^{-6}\,\text{m}^2 \) and absorbs 50\% of the incident light, which is at wavelength \( 600\,\text{nm} \). The detector faces an isotropic source, \( 12.0\,\text{m} \) from the source. The energy \( E \) emitted by the source versus time \( t \) is given in figure \( (E_s = 7.2\,\text{nJ},\,t_s = 2.0\,\text{s}) \). At what rate are photons absorbed by the detector?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the emitted energy versus time is given in the figure
The needed quantitative relation appears to come from a graph, not fully specified in text
image_manifest shows 1 registered image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: Solving likely requires extracting source emission behavior from the figure, then applying isotropic spreading to the detector area
The question also requires using the 50% absorption factor and converting optical energy at 600 nm into photon count rate
This combines graph reading with physics formulas rather than a single direct lookup
Multiple numeric parameters are provided: area, absorption fraction, wavelength, distance, and graph scales
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a specific numeric result ('6.0 photons/s')
choices are provided, giving an additional closed-set check
The result can be checked by a physics-based calculation if the figure is interpreted consistently
Image quality summary reports readability_score=0.788, suggesting the figure is likely usable for verification

verification depends on correct interpretation of the figure's energy-vs-time shape
